title: cassia katangensis
intro: Cassia katangensis, also known as the Katanga senna, is a plant species that belongs to the Fabaceae family and is native to tropical Africa. The plant's leaves and pods have been traditionally used for their medicinal properties, particularly as a laxative due to their high concentration of anthraquinones, which stimulate bowel movements and relieve constipation. Cassia katangensis has been found to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, fighting against bacterial infections, and exhibiting antifungal properties. The plant is commonly prepared as a tea or decoction by infusing the leaves or pods in hot water, and can also be used in powder form or as a capsule supplement, although its use should be supervised by a healthcare professional due to potential side effects.
